programme.title: '60s Pop, Rock & Soul (My Music Presents)
programme.desc: Artists and groups from the 1960s perform, including Davy Jones of The Monkees, Paul Revere & the Raiders, The Miracles and Peter Noone.

programme.title: 60s and 70s Soul Celebration (My Music Presents)
programme.desc: Legends of 1960s and 1970s soul music perform their biggest hits; acts include The Temptations, James Brown, Patti LaBelle, The Chi-Lites, The Spinners, Sam Moore and more.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Mapping the roots of actress Kate Burton and media mogul Barry Diller.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Tiffany Studios lily sconces circa 1905; a 1904 Philadelphia quilt; a Chinese and Japanese decorative art collection.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When one of the world's rarest orchids is smuggled illegally into Midsomer Malham, it triggers a catalogue of passion, jealousy and death.

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Mike, Kristin, and the team look into the murder of a farmer's market owner, who staggers into the crowd with a garden implement embedded into her back.

programme.title: Austin City Limits
programme.desc: Country Music Hall of Famer Tanya Tucker sings songs from "Sweet Western Sound"; Brittney Spencer performs songs from her debut "My Stupid Life.".

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação
Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía

programme.title: Songs at the Center
programme.sub-title: Kelly Willis; Brennen Leigh; Melissa Carper
programme.desc: Songwriters Kelly Willis, Brennen Leigh and Melissa Carper create a unique sound with harmonies and melodies played on guitar, mandolin and bass.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Mapping the roots of actress Kate Burton and media mogul Barry Diller.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When one of the world's rarest orchids is smuggled illegally into Midsomer Malham, it triggers a catalogue of passion, jealousy and death.

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Part 1/2. Foyle's investigation of a body on a deserted beach poses a threat to American aid donations.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.desc: It's Backwards Day in the Treeborhood; Cece, Carly and Clyde have fun with patterns.

programme.title: Wild Kratts: Our Blue and Green World
programme.desc: While doing their annual Laundry Day, the Kratt brothers disagree on what is better -- blue oceans or green forests.

programme.title: Alma's Way
programme.sub-title: Howard Brings the Beat; Alma's Payday
programme.desc: Alma helps Howard find a drum when he joins the neighborhood drum circle; Alma and Junior try to earn toy tickets.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Mapping the roots of actress Kate Burton and media mogul Barry Diller.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Alphy and Geordie's investigation into the murder of an archaeologist uncovers a web of betrayal and deceit.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Alphy is stunned to discover that a woman he knows has gone missing; when her friend is later brutally murdered, Alphy and Geordie race to find the answers.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Geordie and Larry are called out to investigate the mysterious death of a reclusive man on the streets of Cambridge; as they investigate the apparent suicide, Alphy and Geordie unravel a series of clues that leads them to a shocking revelation.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: A suspicious death interrupts the Easter celebrations in Grantchester, and Alphy uses the case as a distraction from his personal life.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: A death at the university plunges Geordie and Alphy into a world of academic adversaries, while Alphy faces a romantic complication.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Alphy's attempt to cook a romantic dinner is interrupted when a familiar face arrives unannounced.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Alphy and Geordie investigate witchcraft at a home for orphaned and disadvantaged chidren, but everything changes when a body is found.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Leonard's drinking lands him in prison overnight, and when he wakes up, the consequences escalate.

programme.title: Washington Week With The Atlantic
programme.desc: Leigh Ann Caldwell, Puck; Stephen Hayes, The Dispatch; Jonathan Lemire, MS Now; Michael Scherer, The Atlantic.

programme.title: Compass Points From PBS News
programme.desc: Experts analyze prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world.

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Part 2/2. Foyle's investigation of a body on a deserted beach poses a threat to American aid donations.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: When tragedy strikes a local family, Dr. Turner's treatment comes under scrutiny; Trixie worries about the future of midwifery in Britain.

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Disaster strikes at Forsyte & Co. as Soames puts an end to Irene's Parisian dreams; Louisa faces a shock eviction.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Edmond continues to infiltrate the lives of his betrayers, uncovering more dark secrets; Mercedes starts to suspect Edmond's true identity.

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days on Masterpiece
programme.desc: While in Hong Kong, Fogg discovers that someone has put a hold on his credit; Passepartout puts his larceny skills to use.

programme.title: Nature Cat: Nature Cat's Nature Movie Special Extraordinaire

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Francine bets Arthur that her family uses less water; Arthur and D.W. believe they will find treasures in the attic that will make them famous.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.desc: When Bex removes her cochlear implant and hearing aid so she can go on the waterslide, Sammy and the Wombats find new ways to communicate; the Wombats love Leiko's new mural.

programme.title: Donkey Hodie
programme.desc: Daniel Tiger and Donkey Hodie meet for the first time in Someplace Else, and Daniel's special visit takes the new pals on a surprising adventure.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.desc: Rosie helps Mom set up for story time when the Purple Knight visits the bookstore; Rosie and Javi make a strawberry horchata stand.

programme.title: Life at the Waterhole
programme.desc: Hotter weather impacts the animals as the waterhole becomes busier as the night gets cooler, and nocturnal activity brings a new predator out of the shadows -- the elusive hyena.

programme.title: Life at the Waterhole
programme.desc: Rain transforms life at the waterhole, creating an abundance of food as mating and birthing seasons arrive; moderate weather makes it harder for predators as prey is able to migrate farther.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Coyote Crossing
programme.desc: Southern California residents and coyotes learn to coexist in the urban landscape.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Items reflect 250 years of American art, artifacts, crafts and collectibles; finds include a flag quilt circa 1880, a Tiffany Studios turtleback glass shade circa 1915 and an 1884 Edison light bulb.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Opposition in colonial North America to British policies over taxes, land and sovereignty escalates into violent confrontation; war gives 13 colonies a common cause.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates the 18th century breakup of Britain and America, including the turning points that led to America's Declaration of Independence from Britain in 1776.

programme.desc: After a groundhog predicts more winter weather, Benny is convinced he will have snow on his birthday so he plans a snow-filled birthday party.

programme.desc: The Loops celebrate the day they met Stu; Liana finds crumbs on Louisa's pillow and accuses her of taking the last cookie.

programme.desc: Lotta competes in the Fuzzytown Music Maker Championship, but the noise on stage becomes too much for her and she leaves; Carl forgets his bottle cap collection at his dad's house.

programme.desc: Rosie and Javi both want to be in charge of the sandbox, so the kids hold an election to choose a president; Rosie and friends try to save the dino parade.
